author: lOrm
content:
Tightened detect_preferences heuristic to reject false positives.

Changes (src/auto_learn.rs):
- New helpers: is_single_imperative_sentence (rejects ? anywhere; rejects multi-sentence), is_substantive_object_phrase (requires >=2 alpha tokens after verb), is_idiomatic_non_preference (denylists 'never mind', 'dont worry', 'always has been', etc.)
- detect_preferences: drops the bare 'use ' prefix (was matching tool invocations like 'use the read_file tool'); gates every preference prefix on is_single_imperative_sentence + is_substantive_object_phrase + is_idiomatic_non_preference
- correction prefixes now require trailing comma/period ('no, ', 'wrong, ', 'wrong. ', 'actually, ', 'instead, ') so 'stop the bash shell' and 'don't use the read_file tool' no longer match

Tests: 13 new fix448_* tests covering the false-positive classes from the issue (question-with-always, use-as-tool-invocation, never-mind idiom, multi-sentence, conditional 'if you always', double-?, prefer-with-multi-sentence) plus positive regressions and direct helper coverage.

Forensic evidence:
- md5(src/auto_learn.rs) = 11a7589e004d4619822e2d9471218206 (stable across full cargo test --lib run)
- cargo test --lib: 1357 passed; 0 failed
- cargo test --lib auto_learn: 24 passed; 0 failed
- cargo clippy --lib --tests: zero diagnostics mention auto_learn.rs
- grep '#[allow|#[ignore]' src/auto_learn.rs: empty
- clippy.toml: absent
